content.full_text:
Re
Farn PolrrrcAl PnecrrcEs C oMMrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 r Sacramento. CA 95814-2329
(916\ 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
ltrly 26,2010
Danian Hopp
Senior Staff Counsel
Legal Affairs Division
Los Angeles Legal Office
320 West Fourth Street, Suite 410
Los Angeles, California 90013
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-10-121
Dear Mr. Hopp:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the reporting provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").' Our advice is based on the facts presented; the Fair Political
Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders advice.
(ln re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any
conduct that has already taken place. Finally, our advice is limited to the provisions of the Act.
Therefore, we do not offer advice on any restrictions that may exist in your incompatible activities
statement or as otherwise set forth in Government Code Section l9gg0.
QUESTIONS
l. Are you required to disclose your private law practice on your Statement of Economic
Interests (Form 700)?
2. Are you required to disclose yollr position as President of the Alta Finisterra
Condominium Association (the "AFCA") on your Statement of Economic Interests (Form 700X
CONCLUSIONS
l. No. Under the facts presented your private law practice is not the type of business
entity that is subject to disclosure pursuant to you disclosure category. However, as the sole
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections tll000 through 91014. r\ll statumry
ret'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections ltt I l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the Califbrnia Co,Je of Regulations. All
regulaklry ret'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califbrnia Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-10-121
Page No. 2
owner of the business, once yollr practice becomes active you will be required to disclose cerlain
clients, as explained below.
2. No. Because the AFCA is a nonprofit corporation, it is not a business entity. Also,
because you serve in a volunteer position, yon do not have a soLlrce of income interest in the
AFCA. Accordingly, you have no economic interest in AFCA.2
FACTS
You are a Senior Legal Counsel with the Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) and are
being considered for a new position. You have also recently established a private law practice
where you hope to attract private clients during your non-state work hours (evenings, weekends,
furlough days, leave days etc.). You have a home office, and you lease a business address at an
executive suite where you can receive mail and meet with clients. You also have a website. You
have yet to receive any income, but hope to be able to eventually eam extra income and build a
private practice to undertake after retirement from state service.
Your position is designated as "Category 2" in the DMV conflict-of-interest code.
Employees designated in that category are required to report:
"A). All investments in, and business positions with, business entities, and
income, including gifts, loans, and travel payments from sources that:
"1. Contract to provide services, supplies, materials, machinery, or
equipment of the type utilized by the Department of Motor Vehicles.
"2. Are of the type subject to occupational licensing by the Department of
Motor vehicles, or which is (sic) subject to Department of Motor vehicle's
regulation.
3. Are an interstate trucking company doing business within the state of
Califomia.
"B). Any interest in real property of the type that may be owned by, or leased or
rented to, the Department of Motor Vehicles."
ANALYSIS
Public officials are required to perform their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias
caused by their own financial interests or the financial interest of others. Toward that end, the
I Even if you did have an economic interest in AFCA. it does not appear to be the type of entity that you
would be required to disclose pursuant to your disclosure category.


File No. A-10-121
Page No. 3
Act establishes certain reporting obligations for public officials3 who are engaged in decision
making. Section 87300 states:
"Every agency shall adopt and promulgate a Conflict of lnterest Code pursuant to
the provisions of this article. A Conflict of Interest Code shall have the force of
law and any violation of a conflict of Interest code by a designated employee
shall be deemed a violation of this chapter."
Section 87302 sets forth the provisions required in an agency's conflict-of-interest code.
"Each Conflict of lnterest Code shall contain the following provisions:
"(a) Specific enumeration of the positions within the agency, other than
those specified in Section 87200, which involve the making or participation in the
making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on any
financial interest and for each such enumerated position, the specific types of
investments, business positions, interests in real property, and sources of income
which are reportable. An investment, business position, interest in real property,
or source of income shall be made reportable by the Conflict of Interest Code if
the business entity in which the investment or business position is held, the
interest in real property, or the income or source of income may foreseeably be
affected materially by any decision made or participated in by the designated
employee by virtue of his or her position.
"(b) Requirements that each designated employee, other than those
specified in Section 87200, file statements at times and under circumstances
described in this section, disclosing reportable investments, business positions,
interests in real property and income."
Finally, Section 87309 provides:
"No Conflict of Interest Code or amendment shall be approved by the
code reviewing body or upheld by a court if it:
"(a) Fails to provide reasonable assurance that all foreseeable potential
conflict of interest situations will be disclosed or prevented;
"(b) Fails to provide to each affected person a clear and specific statement
of his duties under the Code; or
"(c) Fails to adequately differentiate between designated employees with
different powers and responsibilities."
t Public otlicials who are required to file statements of economic interests are either identifled in Section
87200 (statutory tilers) or designated in an agency's conflict-ot'-interest code (designated employees or code fllers


File No. A-10-121
Page No. 4
Regulation 18730 further clarifies the required provisions of a conflict-of-interest code.
Subdivision (b) states:
"(b) The terms of a conflict of interest code amended or adopted and
promulgated pursuant to this regulation are as follows:
"{ ...}
"(2) Section 2. Designated Employees
"The persons holding positions listed in the Appendix are designated
employees. It has been determined that these persons make or participate in the
making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on economic
interests."
A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section
87103 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his or her
immediate family, or on any one of five enumerated economic interests, including:
An economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a direct or
indirect investment of $2,000 or more (Section 87103(a); Regulation 18703.1(a));
or in which he or she is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any
position of management. (Section 87103(d); Regulation 18703.1(b).)
An economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or indirect
interest of $2,000 or more. (Section 87103(b); Regulation 18703.2.)
An economic interest in any source of income, including promised income,
aggregating $500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (section
87 103(c); Regulation 1 8703.3.)
An economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if the gifts aggregate to
$420 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(e);
Regulation 18703.4.)
a
a
a
a
a
An economic interest in his or her personal finances, including those of his or her
immediate family -- this is the "personal financial effects" rule. (Section 87103;
Regulation 18703.5.)
According to the facts you have provided, you have an economic interest in your private
law practice because, as you confirmed in our telephone conversation of July 22,2010, you have
a direct investment of $2,000 or more, and additionally, because yolr are either "a director,
officer, partner, trustee, employee, or Ihold al position of management" in the business entity.a
+ A "business entity" is defined as "any or-ganization or enterprise operated fbr profit." (Section g2005.)


File No. A-10-121
Page No. 5
You also stated that you currently do not have any clients and have received no income
from the practice. Accordingly, you do not have a source of income economic interest in the
business.
You have not provided any other facts relating to your potential economic interests. Our
analysis, therefore, is limited to the information you have provided.
Disclosure
Law Practice: As a Category 2 fller, you are required to disclose all investments in, and
business positions with, business entities, and income from sources that: (l) contract to provide
services, supplies, materials, machinery, or equipment of the type utilized by the Department of
Motor Vehicles; (2) are of the type subject to occupational licensing by the Department of Motor
Vehicles, or subject to Department of Motor Vehicle's regulation; and (3) are an interstate
trucking company doing business within the State of California.
Because your law practice is not subject to regulation by the DMV and does not fall
within the other categories listed, you are not required to disclose your economic interest in your
law practice nor the income you expect to receive from you practice once you start accepting
clients. However, because your practice is a sole proprietorship, you have an interest of l0-
percent or greater in the law practice. Income, includes "a pro rata share of any income of any
businessentityinwhichthe individual ... owns... a lO-percentinterestorgreater." (Section
82030.) Accordingly, once you start accepting clients, these clients will be sources of income to
you.
For reporting purposes Section 87207 (b) provides:
"(b) When the filer's pro rata share of income to a business entity,
including income to a sole proprietorship, is required to be reported under this
article, the statement shall contain:
"(1) The name, address, and a general description of the business activity
of the business entity.
"(2) The name of every person from whom the business entity received
payments if the filer's pro rata share of gross receipts from that person was equal
to or greater than ten thousand dollars ($10,000) during a calendar year.',
Therefore, you must identify any individual who is a source of income to you and from
whom you receive $10,000 or more in payments if that source is subject to DMV regulation.
This would include any individual client with a California Driver's License.s
' Please be aware that for conflict-of'-interest disqualifrcation, the threshold is $500 or more. as stated
above.


File No. A-10-121
Page No. 6
AFCA: You hold a position of management with AFCA. However, because AFCA is a
nonprofit organization, it does not meet the definition of "business entity."
Furthermore, because you are serving in a volunteer capacity, you receive no income for your
service. Therefore, AFCA is neither a "business position" nor a "source of income" that is
required to be disclose on Schedule C of your Form 700. Furtheffnore, even if it did qualify as
an economic interest (for example, if you started drawing a salary or otherwise received a
payment that qualifies as income) you would not have to report this source pursuant to your
disclosure category.
If you have other qllestions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
\
By
Lenkeit
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
WJL:jgl
